title: Faithful: PAEBadTV param-response diverges from headless FCP (worst ddb 11.3 dB)
goal: FAITHFUL-REIMPL (ROADMAP Rule 13): the TS engine impl of PAEBadTV must match REAL headless FCP across its ENTIRE parameter space, not just values the 65 fixed transitions use. The DELTA oracle (fct/faithful) set each continuous param of PAEBadTV to range extremes across hosts ['Lights__Static'] and compared how the oracle vs the engine RESPOND: ddb=PSNR(oracle(theta)-oracle(theta0), engine(theta)-engine(theta0)). worst ddb=11.29 < pass 40 means the engine responds WRONGLY to a real parameter change (a faithful engine scores ~99 dB; the identity/noise floor is 99). Fix the PAEBadTV implementation so its parameter response matches FCP. NO scene-signature dispatch, NO env flags (Rule 12/13): behavior = f(filter params) only. Re-verify: `python3 -m fct.faithful.driver sweep PAEBadTV` must reach >= pass_db worst-ddb across all hosts. Worst diverging (host/param/theta -> ddb): [{"host": "Lights__Static", "param": "mix", "kind": "curve", "theta": 0.5, "t": 0.1, "oracle_signal": 69.483, "ddb": 11.293}, {"host": "Lights__Static", "param": "mix", "kind": "curve", "theta": 0.5, "t": 0.5, "oracle_signal": 5.86, "ddb": 32.773}]
